title: Shiplap Wreath Display Frame
summary: This is a quick and easy build.
description: This is a quick and easy build. Perfect for beginners! This wreath display frame is designed to look like SHIPLAP, so it's perfectly rustic and fits with any farmhouse or industrial decor. While it's great for wreaths, you can display anything on it.

steps.title: Cut Support Pieces
steps.body: Place all of your planks together in a row. Measure the width of your planked pieces (across the planks). It should be about 25". Cut two 1x3's to match that measurement.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the ends of the two support boards.

steps.title: Cut Frame Pieces
steps.body: Cut 2 1x2's at the same length as your support pieces (that you drilled pocket holes in)- should be about 25 inches. Measure the width of the planked pieces including the frame pieces you just cut, and cut 2 1x2's at that length (should be about 26") see diagram Do not attach any pieces yet.

steps.title: Dry Fit
steps.body: We should have everything cut and ready to dry fit. Make sure all of the boards fit together squarely and that the frame pieces match up.

steps.title: Attach Frame
steps.body: Using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue, attach the support pieces to the frame.

steps.title: Attach Suppports to Planks
steps.body: Using a brad nailer and 1 1/4" brad nails, attach the support pieces to the backs of your planks. Use wood glue for extra strength. Since we will be attaching our frame to our 1x3 support pieces, make sure the ends of your support pieces are flush with the sides of your 1x3s. I suggest attaching one side of the planks, then the other side, and work your way inward as you attach each piece.

steps.title: Assemble Frame
steps.body: Use wood glue at every joint, and assemble the frame in place. Use bar clamps to ensure the frame doesn't wiggle.

steps.title: Paint or Stain
steps.body: After all of your pieces are cut and fit, paint or stain all of the pieces.

steps.title: Install Picture Hanger
steps.body: Install whatever picture hanger you prefer. Since this is a heavy piece to hang on the wall, I used a heavy gauge wire, attached to each support piece with a staple.

steps.title: Decorate!
steps.body: I hammered a simple nail in the front of my wreath display. Then I just hang whatever wreath or decoration I want right on the front.
